Korea n companies have their power quality inspected by Care Lab s. Our aim is to stop your electrical systems, equipment, and appliances from breaking down because of unstable power supplies. Energy-sensitive data gathering, monitoring, and instrumentation systems are deployed by several organizations and an increasing number of private consumers. With distributed energy, a broad variety of voltage variations can occur, yet few electrical products are designed to handle them. Most electrical equipment is built to handle certain supply variances. Concerns about power quality, such as equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning, can be helped by Care Lab s’ professionals.
The following is the aim of power quality analysis:

Every electrical network experience power outage, which happens more frequently when power lines are damaged by storms, natural catastrophes, or accidents. It is normal to switch the source of one’s electrical energy.
Distribution of power from an electrical network changes, interruptions, and distortions fall into three categories:
1. When a frequency deviates from its native frequency range, this is called a frequency deviation.
2.Voltage disturbances happen when the form of the voltage is consistent, but the amplitude is greater than the normal range.
2.1 Voltage dips and spikes that last up to a minute are examples of short-term variations.
2.2 Over voltages and Under voltages are examples of longer-lasting fluctuations.
2.3 Unbalanced voltage: This condition develops when the load is not distributed equally across the phases, which causes the equipment to overheat.
2.4 Voltage difference between the ground and neutral
3. Disturbances brought on by distortion, which occurs when the voltage’s shape is not even or smooth.
3.1 Electronic equipment is susceptible to overheating, power loss, poor power utilization, and resonance issues due to harmonics and inter harmonics.

3.2 Short-lived transients that last less than a tenth of a second have the potential to harm switch contact surfaces, lower system performance, and hasten the failure of motor drives and control systems.
